preamble: A shift from an alveolar liquid [r] to a labio-velar approximant [w], often seen in child language development or specific non-rhotic/dialectal varieties of English.

examples.note: Labialization of [r] common in various English dialects.
Common developmental substitution during phonetic acquisition.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
